market.question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?
market.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?

assumptions.assumption: Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei (age 86 in 2026) remains alive and in control, maintaining the current regime's structural opposition to a permanent peace deal with the US.
assumptions.breakingEvent: Death or incapacitation of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, triggering a succession crisis and potential regime shift
assumptions.timeToBreak: Any time before June 15, 2026; most consequential if it occurs 3-12 months before resolution (mid-2025 to early 2026)

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?

assumptions.assumption: Iran's Supreme Leader (Khamenei, age 86) remains alive and in control of Iran's nuclear and foreign policy decision-making through June 2026, maintaining the current regime's structural opposition to a permanent peace deal with the US.
assumptions.breakingEvent: Death or incapacitation of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, or a leadership succession crisis in Iran that brings a more moderate faction to power willing to negotiate a permanent peace deal.
assumptions.timeToBreak: Any time between now and June 15, 2026 (resolution date)

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?

assumptions.assumption: Iran's Supreme Leader (Khamenei, age 86) remains alive and in control of Iran's nuclear and foreign policy decision-making through June 2026.
assumptions.breakingEvent: Death or incapacitation of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, triggering a succession crisis and power struggle that would make any permanent peace deal impossible during the transition period.
assumptions.timeToBreak: Any time before June 15, 2026; most fragile if occurring within 3-6 months of the deadline

assumptions.assumption: The US and Iran will not reach a permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026, because the structural obstacles (nuclear program, regional proxies, sanctions, domestic politics) are too deep to resolve in this timeframe.
assumptions.breakingEvent: The two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026 is extended or converted into a framework for permanent negotiations before it expires around April 21, 2026.
assumptions.timeToBreak: April 21, 2026 (approximately 55 days before resolution, when the temporary ceasefire expires)

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?

assumptions.assumption: Iran's Supreme Leader (Khamenei, age 86) remains alive and in power through June 2026, maintaining his long-standing opposition to permanent normalization with the US.
assumptions.breakingEvent: Death or incapacitation of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, triggering a leadership transition that could fundamentally alter Iran's negotiating posture
assumptions.timeToBreak: Any time before June 15, 2026 - most likely scenario would be sudden due to age/health

assumptions.assumption: The US and Iran are on a trajectory toward a permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026, rather than escalating conflict or remaining in a state of no agreement.
assumptions.breakingEvent: The expiration or collapse of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026, without a permanent deal replacing it.
assumptions.timeToBreak: April 21, 2026 (14 days after the temporary ceasefire was announced)

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?

assumptions.assumption: Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei (age 85+) remains alive and in control to authorize a permanent peace deal, or if he dies, his successor is equally willing to negotiate such a deal with the US.
assumptions.breakingEvent: Death or incapacitation of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, or a succession crisis that empowers hardliners opposed to any permanent peace with the US
assumptions.timeToBreak: Any time before June 15, 2026; Khamenei's health is a continuous unknown

assumptions.assumption: The current US-Iran ceasefire (announced April 7, 2026) will hold and serve as a foundation that can be extended or converted into a permanent deal by June 15, 2026.
assumptions.breakingEvent: A significant violation of the April 7 ceasefire by either side (e.g., attack on military assets, nuclear facility incident, or IRGC/Quds Force engagement)
assumptions.timeToBreak: Any day between now and June 15, 2026

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?

assumptions.assumption: Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei (age 86 as of 2025) remains alive and in effective control of Iran's nuclear and foreign policy decision-making through June 2026
assumptions.breakingEvent: Death or incapacitation of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, triggering a succession crisis and likely freezing any permanent peace negotiations
assumptions.timeToBreak: Any time before June 15, 2026; Khamenei's health is a continuous fragility

assumptions.assumption: The US and Iran will not reach a permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026, because the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026 is explicitly temporary and does not signal a lasting end to hostilities.
assumptions.breakingEvent: The April 7, 2026 two-week ceasefire expires without being extended or converted into a permanent deal, confirming the temporary nature of the agreement and eliminating any near-term path to a permanent deal.
assumptions.timeToBreak: April 21, 2026 (expiration of the two-week ceasefire announced April 7)

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?

assumptions.assumption: Iran's nuclear program has not advanced to weapons-grade enrichment levels that would make a permanent peace deal politically impossible for the US administration to sell domestically by June 2026.
assumptions.breakingEvent: IAEA Board of Governors resolution referring Iran to the UN Security Council for non-compliance, or IAEA report confirming Iran has enriched uranium to 90% (weapons-grade) or accumulated enough near-weapons-grade material for a nuclear device.
assumptions.timeToBreak: Next IAEA quarterly report expected ~February/March 2026, with potential Board of Governors meeting ~March 2026 — roughly 2-3 months before resolution date.

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?

assumptions.assumption: Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei (age 86, with reported health issues) remains alive and in control through June 2026, as his death or incapacitation would likely derail any permanent deal negotiations.
assumptions.breakingEvent: Death or incapacitation of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, or credible reports of his serious health decline

assumptions.assumption: The current geopolitical trajectory of US-Iran relations will continue without a dramatic, unexpected breakthrough toward a permanent peace deal before June 15, 2026.
assumptions.breakingEvent: The US and Iran announce resumption of formal negotiations toward a comprehensive peace agreement, with both sides publicly committing to a timeline before June 15, 2026.
assumptions.timeToBreak: Any time between now and June 15, 2026 — but earliest potential is the next round of diplomatic engagement (possibly as soon as the next UN General Assembly session or a bilateral meeting announced in the coming months)

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?

assumptions.assumption: Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei (age 86, with reported health issues) remains alive and in effective control of Iran's nuclear and foreign policy decision-making through June 2026.
assumptions.breakingEvent: Death or incapacitation of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, triggering a succession crisis and likely freezing any permanent diplomatic agreements.
assumptions.timeToBreak: Any time before June 15, 2026; Khamenei's health is a continuous risk factor

assumptions.assumption: The US and Iran will not reach a permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026, because the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026 is explicitly temporary and does not signal a lasting end to hostilities.
assumptions.breakingEvent: The April 7, 2026 two-week ceasefire expires without being extended or converted into a permanent deal, confirming the temporary nature of the agreement and eliminating any near-term pathway to a permanent peace deal.
assumptions.timeToBreak: April 21, 2026 (when the two-week ceasefire announced April 7 expires)

assumptions.assumption: The current trajectory of US-Iran relations is linear and reversible, meaning that the escalation-de-escalation cycle of the past decades can be broken by a single comprehensive agreement rather than requiring a fundamental restructuring of both countries' political incentives and regional security architectures.
assumptions.breakingEvent: A major Iranian retaliation against Israel or US forces in the region (e.g., using advanced missiles or proxy forces) that occurs after a period of positive diplomatic signaling, demonstrating that the two countries' regional proxy conflicts operate on independent tracks from bilateral negotiations.
assumptions.timeToBreak: Any point between now and June 2026, but most likely during a period of apparent diplomatic progress when a regional incident (strike on Iranian facility, proxy escalation in Yemen/Syria/Iraq, or Israeli operation) triggers an Iranian response that the US cannot ignore

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?

assumptions.assumption: Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei (age 86 as of 2026) remains alive and in control of Iran's nuclear and foreign policy decision-making through June 15, 2026.
assumptions.breakingEvent: Death, incapacitation, or removal of Ayatollah Khamenei as Supreme Leader, triggering a succession crisis or shift in Iran's negotiating posture
assumptions.timeToBreak: Any time before June 15, 2026; most fragile if occurring within 3-6 months of the deadline

assumptions.assumption: The US and Iran will not reach a permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026, because the structural geopolitical barriers (nuclear program, regional proxies, sanctions regime) are too deep to resolve in this timeframe.
assumptions.breakingEvent: The two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026, is extended or converted into a more durable framework before its expiration around April 21, 2026.

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?

opinions.name: Fundamentals Analyst
opinions.reasoning: [high] Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei (age 86 in 2026) remains alive and in control, maintaining the current regime's structural opposition to a permanent peace deal with the US. | Death or incapacitation of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, triggering a succession crisis and potential regime shift

opinions.name: Event Horizon
opinions.reasoning: [high] The US and Iran will not reach a permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026, because the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026 is explicitly temporary and the structural obstacles to a permanent deal remain insurmountable. | The two-week ceasefire (announced April 7, 2026) expires around April 21, 2026, and either (a) both sides announce a permanent peace deal before the ceasefire expires, or (b) the ceasefire is extended with language signaling a permanent end to hostilities, or (c) the ceasefire collapses and hostilities resume, confirming the consensus.

opinions.name: Contrarian
opinions.reasoning: [high] The current US-Iran ceasefire (announced April 7, 2026) represents a genuine de-escalation pathway that both sides intend to build upon toward a permanent deal, rather than a tactical pause both are using to rearm and reposition for renewed conflict. | Any reported violation of the April 7 ceasefire by either side, or statements by either government accusing the other of using the ceasefire to rearm or reposition forces.
